name: The Girl From Your Dreams
description:
(Author's Note: I was bored this morning, Enjoy!)

You can still recall the first dream she showed up in. You were only age 5 yourself, but had a dream you were playing in a sandbox, and she came and sat down and played with you, a young girl of about 16, willow thin, with hair so blonde it was practically white. From the first you never questioned why she was there, or who she was - her being there always felt....right. She never told you her name, but you knew somehow it was Morgan.

At least every few months you would dream of her again, always familiar, always sweet and genuine. A dream of her exclaiming at the crayfish you caught in a dream of a creek when you are 8. A dream of talking to her about your first crush when you were 10. A dream of telling her how you broke your arm when you were 12. Crying to her the first time your heart got broken at 15. As you turned 17, you somehow sense that time had started to move forward for her as well, and she began to age along with you. Every dream your conversations and topics got deeper. Your connection to her grew. Despite only existing in a dream, she was one of your closest friends.

When you were 18, you had the last dream of her you would ever have: you dreamed of being in college, Morgan was also. She was your partner. You both graduate. Your relationship is,,,happy. Time jumps forward in fits and starts in the manner of most dreams. First apartment together. Marriage. Children. Careers. You grow old together, feeling a contentment you have never known, and most people only dream of. Your kids have kids. Old age sets in. Morgan goes first - in her sleep. All you can remember of the funeral was thinking that you were not far behind her. A few nights later, you settle into bed drifting off. You are awaked some time later by someone sitting on the edge of the bed. You open your eyes, and it's Morgan, only she is a young girl again. She smiles as you wake, and reaches down to caress your cheek. She stands, and offers you her hand to stand with her. You stand, as she turns and leads you towards your closed closet door. She opens it, and a blinding white light streams out of it, making it almost impossible to see. She steps forward and you step with her. As the door closes, you risk a look backwards at the bed, seeing your own motionless form still lying there. As the door clicks shut behind you, you....wake up.

You look around in shock for a few seconds, trying to process the sudden change in surroundings. You sigh loudly, as you realize you are back in the real world. You lay on your back staring at the ceiling, when it suddenly all comes back to you in a near instant: your marriage, your kids, watching them grow up, watching them become adults, growing old, having grandkids bouncing on your knee, your beautiful loving Morgan always by your side - a life long, happy and well-lived. You cannot stave off the tears that follow. You simply weep. Gasping, uncontrollable sobs for the loss of a life you will never have. You finally manage a fitful couple of hours of sleep, mostly due to being exhausted from crying. You spend a month in kind of a fog, unable to shake off the dream. Every time you go to sleep you hope you will see Morgan again, to see if she remembers the life you once lived together, but...you never see her again.

Over the years the details of the dream fade, but you never forget Morgan. You never forget her constant warm, loving presence in your life - even if it was only in dreams. You hope that one day you might dream of her again.
creator: whatnooneeverknew
